translations.idiomatic: Amnesty brought him back; competing summonses sent him first to the censorate as clerk.

sentences.zh: 宣帝識之，拜為平陵令，坐殺不辜，去官。

translations.literal: Emperor Xuan recognized him—appointed Pingling chief—sat killing innocents—removed office.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Xuan knew him and named him magistrate of Pingling until wrongful executions cost him the post.

sentences.zh: 後為丞相掾，復擢好畤令。

translations.literal: Later chancellor clerk—again promoted Haozhi chief.
translations.idiomatic: He returned as chancellor clerk and rose to magistrate of Haozhi.

sentences.zh: 神爵中，西羌反，強弩將軍許延壽請延年為長史，從軍敗西羌，還為涿郡太守。

translations.literal: In Shenjue era Western Qiang rebelled—Strong Crossbow General Xu Yanshou requested Yannian as chief clerk—followed army defeating Western Qiang—returned Zhuo grand administrator.
translations.idiomatic: During the Qiang wars he served Xu Yanshou as chief clerk, helped smash the tribes, and became governor of Zhuo.

sentences.zh: 時，郡比得不能太守，涿人畢野白等由是廢亂。

translations.literal: At the time the commandery repeatedly obtained incapable grand administrators—Zhuo people Bi Yebo and others thereby ruined chaotic.
translations.idiomatic: Zhuo had suffered inept governors until locals like Bi Yebo ran wild.

sentences.zh: 大姓西高氏、東高氏，自郡吏以下皆畏避之，莫敢與校，咸曰：「寧負二千石，無負豪大家。

translations.literal: Great clans West Gao and East Gao—from commandery clerks downward all feared avoided—none dared contend—all said: "Better betray two-thousand-bushel—never betray great magnate houses.
translations.idiomatic: The rival Gao houses terrified every official with the proverb: "Snub the governor before you snub a magnate."

sentences.zh: 」賓客放為盜賊，發，輒入高氏，吏不敢追。

translations.literal: "Guests unrestrained acting robbers-thieves—when exposed—always fled into Gao clan—clerks dared not pursue.
translations.idiomatic: Their clients robbed with impunity and vanished into Gao compounds beyond police reach.

sentences.zh: 浸浸日多，道路張弓拔刃，然後敢行，其亂如此。

translations.literal: Gradually daily more—on roads they drew bows and drew blades—then dared travel—such was the chaos.
translations.idiomatic: Robbery mounted until travelers armed themselves before stepping abroad.

sentences.zh: 延年至，遣掾蠡吾趙繡按高氏得其死罪。

translations.literal: When Yannian arrived—sent clerk Zhao Xiu of Liwu to investigate High clan—obtained their capital crimes.
translations.idiomatic: On arrival he dispatched Zhao Xiu of Liwu to nail the Gao houses on capital charges.

sentences.zh: 繡見延年新將，心內懼，即為兩劾，欲先白其輕者觀延年意，怒，乃出其重劾。

translations.literal: Xiu seeing Yannian newly appointed—inwardly feared—made two indictments—wished first report lighter watching Yannian's mood—angry—then produced heavy indictment.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Xiu, nervous under the new governor, drafted light and heavy counts and tested which Yannian wanted.

sentences.zh: 延年已知其如此矣。

translations.literal: Yannian already knew he would do thus.
translations.idiomatic: Yan Yannian saw through the dodge.

sentences.zh: 趙掾至，果白其輕者，延年索懷中，得重劾，即收送獄。

translations.literal: When Clerk Zhao arrived—indeed reported lighter—Yannian searched bosom—found heavy indictment—immediately seized sent jail.
translations.idiomatic: When Zhao offered the mild charges, Yan searched his robe for the harsh draft and jailed him.

sentences.zh: 夜入，晨將至市論殺之，先所按者死，吏皆股弁。

translations.literal: Entered by night—by dawn led to market sentenced killed—those previously investigated died—clerks all thigh trembled.
translations.idiomatic: He dragged the Gaos out before dawn for execution; every clerk shook.

sentences.zh: 更遣吏分考兩高，窮竟其奸，誅殺各數十人。

translations.literal: Further sent clerks separately interrogate two Gao houses—exhausted their villainy—executed killed several tens each.
translations.idiomatic: He split squads to rake both Gao factions and executed dozens from each.

sentences.zh: 郡中震恐，道不拾遺。

translations.literal: Within commandery shook in terror—road none picked up lost.
translations.idiomatic: Zhuo trembled into silence; lost goods lay untouched.

sentences.zh: 三歲，遷河南太守，賜黃金二十斤。

translations.literal: Third year—promoted Henan grand administrator—granted twenty jin yellow gold.
translations.idiomatic: Three years later he governed Henan with twenty catties of gold.

sentences.zh: 豪強脅息，野無行盜，威震旁郡。

translations.literal: Powerful houses held breath—wilds without roaming robbers—awe shook neighboring commanderies.
translations.idiomatic: Magnates went quiet, bandits vanished from the countryside, and neighboring governors felt his shadow.

sentences.zh: 其治務在摧折豪強，扶助貧弱。

translations.literal: His governance strove to crush powerful houses—assist poor weak.
translations.idiomatic: His program smashed magnates while lifting the downtrodden.

sentences.zh: 貧弱雖陷法，曲文以出之；

translations.literal: Poor weak though trapped in law—twisted documents to release them;
translations.idiomatic: Petty offenders saw statutes bent to free them;

sentences.zh: 其豪傑侵小民者，以文內之。

translations.literal: those heroes bullying common people—used documents to net them.
translations.idiomatic: powerful bullies met indictments twisted to trap them.

sentences.zh: 眾人所謂當死者，一朝出之；

translations.literal: What crowd deemed ought die—one morning released;
translations.idiomatic: Men the mob called doomed walked free overnight;

sentences.zh: 所謂當生者，詭殺之。

translations.literal: what deemed ought live—deviously killed.
translations.idiomatic: those slated for mercy died by legal sleight.

sentences.zh: 吏民莫能測其意深淺，戰慄不敢犯禁。

translations.literal: Clerks and people none could fathom his intent depth—trembled dared not violate prohibitions.
translations.idiomatic: No one could read his whims; fear kept every law.

sentences.zh: 按其獄，皆文致不可得反。

translations.literal: Pressing his jail cases—all documents wrought beyond reversal.
translations.idiomatic: His case files closed so tight no appeal could pry them.

sentences.zh: 延年為人短小精悍，敏捷於事，雖子貢、冉有通藝於政事，不能絕也。

translations.literal: Yannian as person short small keen fierce—nimble in affairs—though Zigong and Ran You penetrated arts in governance—could not surpass.
translations.idiomatic: Small and fierce, he outpaced even Zigong and Ran You in bureaucratic speed.

sentences.zh: 吏忠盡節者，厚遇之如骨肉，皆親鄉之，出身不顧，以是治下無隱情。

translations.literal: Clerks loyal exhausting integrity—he treated thickly like flesh and bone—all drew near—throwing body heedless—thereby governing below without hidden facts.
translations.idiomatic: He cherished loyal subordinates like kin until none hid intelligence from him.

sentences.zh: 然疾惡泰甚，中傷者多，尤巧為獄文，善史書，所欲誅殺，奏成於手，中主簿親近史不得聞知。

translations.literal: Yet hated evil excessively—those slandered many—especially crafty in jail documents—good at clerical script—those wished kill—memorial completed in hand—chief clerk intimate clerks could not hear know.
translations.idiomatic: Yet his zeal bred slander; he drafted lethal memorials alone in flawless clerical hand.

sentences.zh: 奏可論死，奄忽如神。

translations.literal: Memorial approved sentenced death—sudden as spirit.
translations.idiomatic: Imperial approval and execution arrived like lightning.

sentences.zh: 冬月，傳屬縣囚，會論府上，流血數里，河南號曰「屠伯」。

translations.literal: Winter month—transferred county prisoners—assembled judging at yamen—blood flowed several li—Henan titled him "Butcher Earl."
translations.idiomatic: Winter sessions piled corpses for miles until Henan dubbed him the Butcher Prefect.

sentences.zh: 令行禁止，郡中正清。

translations.literal: Orders issued prohibitions ceased—within commandery centrally pure.
translations.idiomatic: Orders bit and crime stalled; the commandery ran clean.

sentences.zh: 是時，張敞為京兆尹，素與延年善。

translations.literal: At this time Zhang Chang was Metropolitan Governor—always with Yannian friendly.
translations.idiomatic: Metropolitan Governor Zhang Chang had long been his friend.

sentences.zh: 敞治雖嚴，然尚頗有縱捨，聞延年用刑刻急，乃以書諭之曰：「昔朝盧之取菟也，上觀下獲，不甚多殺。

translations.literal: Chang's governance, though stern, still showed some lenience; hearing that Yannian applied punishments in a harsh and pressing way, he wrote instructing him: "When the Han Lu hound catches rabbits, the master watches from above while the dog catches below—it does not involve mass killing."
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Chang ran a tight but not blood-soaked capital; learning of Yan's rush to execute, he urged the finesse of a royal hunt—fewer corpses, more control.

sentences.zh: 願次卿少緩誅罰，思行此術。

translations.literal: Wish Ciqing slightly slack executions—ponder practicing this art.
translations.idiomatic: He begged Yan Yannian to ease the blade.

sentences.zh: 」延年報曰：「河南天下喉咽，二周余斃，莠盛苗穢，何可不鋤也？

translations.literal: "Yannian replied: "Henan is realm's throat—two Zhou's leavings perish—weeds flourish seedlings foul—how can we not hoe?"
translations.idiomatic: Yan shot back that Henan was the empire's throat and weeds had to burn.

sentences.zh: 」自矜伐其能，終不衰止。

translations.literal: "Self-praising his ability—finally never slackened.
translations.idiomatic: He never trimmed his pride or his killings.

sentences.zh: 時，黃霸在穎川以寬恕為治，郡中亦平，屢蒙豐年，鳳皇下，上賢焉，下詔稱揚其行，加金爵之賞。

translations.literal: At that time Huang Ba in Yingchuan governed by lenience—within commandery also calm—repeatedly received bumper years—phoenix descended—sovereign deemed worthy—issued edict praising conduct—added gold-noble reward.
translations.idiomatic: Meanwhile Huang Ba's gentle Yingchuan drew phoenix omens and imperial gold.

sentences.zh: 延年素輕霸為人，及比郡為守，褒賞反在己前，心內不服。

translations.literal: Yannian always slighted Ba's person—when neighboring commanderies both grand administrators—praise reward fell before himself—inwardly unconvinced.
translations.idiomatic: Yan despised Huang Ba yet watched him praised ahead of himself.

sentences.zh: 河南界中又有蝗蟲，府丞義出行蝗，還見延年，延年曰：「此蝗豈鳳皇食邪？

translations.literal: Within Henan boundary locusts again swarmed—assistant Yi went inspecting locusts—returned seeing Yannian—Yannian said: "These locusts—could phoenix eat them?"
translations.idiomatic: When locusts swarmed Henan he sneered whether phoenixes ate insects.

sentences.zh: 」義又道司農中丞耿壽昌為常平倉，利百姓，延年曰：「丞相御史不知為也，當避位去。

translations.literal: "Yi further spoke Excellency of Agriculture vice-clerk Geng Shouchang making Ever-Normal Granary—benefiting hundred surnames—Yannian said: "Chancellor and imperial clerks not knowing to do—ought yield seats depart.
translations.idiomatic: He raged that grain reforms belonged to chancellor and censor, not agrarian clerks.

sentences.zh: 壽昌安得權此？

translations.literal: How does Shouchang obtain authority here?"
translations.idiomatic: How dare Geng Shouchang seize such power?"

sentences.zh: 」後左馮翊缺，上欲征延年，符已發，為其名酷復止。

translations.literal: "Later Zuo Fengyi lacked—sovereign wished summon Yannian—credentials already issued—because his name cruel again stopped.
translations.idiomatic: A Zuo Fengyi vacancy nearly summoned him until his cruelty canceled the edict.

sentences.zh: 延年疑少府梁丘賀毀之，心恨。

translations.literal: Yannian suspected Privy Treasurer Liangqiu He slandered him—inwardly hated.
translations.idiomatic: He blamed Liangqiu He for blackening his name.

sentences.zh: 會琅邪太守以視事久病，滿三月免，延年自知見廢，謂丞曰：「此人尚能去官，我反不能去邪？

translations.literal: When Langye grand administrator because assuming office long ill—full three months dismissed—Yannian knew himself discarded—said assistant: "This man still able leave office—I reverse cannot leave?"
translations.idiomatic: Seeing another governor cashiered for sick leave, he fumed that even fools could quit.

sentences.zh: 」又延年察獄史廉，有臧不入身，延年坐選舉不實貶秩，笑曰：「後敢復有舉人者矣！

translations.literal: "Further Yannian inspected jail clerk honest—had bribery not entering himself—Yannian sat recommendation untrue demoted salary—laughed saying: "Later dare again have recommenders!"
translations.idiomatic: When an honest nomination cost him rank he mocked ever recommending men.

sentences.zh: 」丞義年老頗悖，素畏延年，恐見中傷。

translations.literal: "Assistant Yi old age rather muddled—always feared Yannian—feared seeing slander.
translations.idiomatic: Old Assistant Yi trembled at imaginary plots.

sentences.zh: 延年本嘗與義俱為丞相史，實親厚之，無意毀傷也，饋遺之甚厚。

translations.literal: Yannian originally once with Yi together were chancellor clerks—truly intimate thick—no intent harm—gifts extremely generous.
translations.idiomatic: Yan had once served beside Yi and showered him with gifts without malice.

sentences.zh: 義愈益恐，自筮得死卦，忽忽不樂，取告至長安，上書言延年罪名十事。

translations.literal: Yi grew more fearful—self-divined obtaining death hex—dazed unhappy—took leave to Chang'an—upward memorial listing ten crimes of Yannian.
translations.idiomatic: Yi cast a death oracle, fled to the capital, and denounced ten crimes.

sentences.zh: 已拜奏，因飲藥自殺，以明不欺。

translations.literal: Already memorial submitted—thereupon drank poison suicide—to clarify no deceit.
translations.idiomatic: He swallowed poison after filing to prove sincerity.

sentences.zh: 事下御史丞按驗，有此數事，以結延年，坐怨望非謗政治不道棄市。

translations.literal: Affair sent vice-censor investigate verify—had these several matters—to nail Yannian—sat resentful slander attacking governance not Way exposed market.
translations.idiomatic: The vice-censor verified the counts and executed Yan Yannian for disloyal railery.

sentences.zh: 初，延年母從東海來，欲從延年臘，到雒陽，適見報囚。

translations.literal: Formerly Yannian's mother came from Donghai—wished join Yannian la sacrifice—arrived Luoyang—happened see reporting prisoners.
translations.idiomatic: His mother arrived for the winter offering and witnessed executions at Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 母大驚，便止都亭，不肯入府。

translations.literal: Mother greatly alarmed—stopped capital relay lodge—refused enter yamen.
translations.idiomatic: Horrified, she halted at the relay inn and refused his compound.

sentences.zh: 延年出至都亭謁母，母閉閣不見。

translations.literal: Yannian went out to capital relay lodge bowed mother—mother barred gate not seeing.
translations.idiomatic: He knelt outside until she opened.

sentences.zh: 延年免冠頓首閣下，良久，母乃見之，因數責延年：「幸得備郡守，專治千里，不聞仁愛教化，有以全安愚民，顧乘刑罰多刑殺人，欲以立威，豈為民父母意哉！

translations.literal: Yannian removed cap knocked head below gate—long time—mother then saw him—thereupon repeatedly reproached Yannian: "Fortunate enough prepare grand administrator—solely governing thousand li—not hearing humane love transforming instruction—having means wholly secure foolish people—yet relying punishments much killing people—wishing thereby establish awe—how is this people's father-mother intent!"
translations.idiomatic: She berated him for slaughtering instead of nurturing like a parent.

sentences.zh: 」延年服罪，重頓首謝，因自為母御，歸府捨。

translations.literal: "Yannian admitted guilt—heavy knocked head apologized—thereupon personally drove for mother—returned residence.
translations.idiomatic: He confessed and drove her carriage home in shame.

sentences.zh: 母畢正臘，謂延年：「天道神明，人不可獨殺。

translations.literal: Mother finished upright la—said Yannian: "Heaven's way spirits bright—person cannot alone kill.
translations.idiomatic: After the rites she warned that Heaven punishes lone killers.

sentences.zh: 我不意當老見壯子被刑戮也！

translations.literal: "I never expected to grow old seeing vigorous son suffer execution!"
translations.idiomatic: She never thought to watch her prime son led to the block.

sentences.zh: 行矣！

translations.literal: "Go!"
translations.idiomatic: Begone!"

sentences.zh: 去女東歸，掃除墓地耳。

translations.literal: Leave you east return—sweep tombs only.
translations.idiomatic: Return east and sweep my grave."

sentences.zh: 」遂去，歸郡，見昆弟宗人，復為言之。

translations.literal: "Thereupon departed—returned commandery—saw brothers clan people—again spoke it.
translations.idiomatic: She left, told the clan in Donghai, and vanished.

sentences.zh: 後歲餘，果敗。

translations.literal: Later slightly more than year—indeed ruined.
translations.idiomatic: Within a year her prophecy held.

sentences.zh: 東海莫不賢知其母。

translations.literal: Donghai none not worthy knew her mother.
translations.idiomatic: Eastern Sea praised his mother's wisdom.

sentences.zh: 延年兄弟五人皆有吏材，至大官，東海號曰「萬石嚴嫗」。

translations.literal: Yannian brothers five all had clerk talent—reached high offices—Donghai titled "Ten-thousand-bushel Yan Matron."
translations.idiomatic: Five brothers rose high; Donghai called their mother the Ten-Thousand-Bushel Matron.

sentences.zh: 次弟彭祖，至太子太傅，在《儒林傳》。

translations.literal: Younger brother Pengzu—reached heir-apparent grand tutor—in Ru Forest biography.
translations.idiomatic: Younger brother Pengzu became heir's tutor—see the Ru scholars chapter.

sentences.zh: 尹賞字子心，巨鹿楊氏人也。

translations.literal: Yin Shang styled Zixin—was a man of Yangshi in Julu.
translations.idiomatic: Yin Shang, courtesy Zixin, came from Yangshi in Julu.

sentences.zh: 以郡吏察廉為樓煩長。

translations.literal: By commandery clerk investigated incorrupt became chief of Loufan.
translations.idiomatic: Recommended honest from county service, he ruled Loufan.

sentences.zh: 舉茂材、粟邑令。

translations.literal: Raised abundant talent—chief of Suyi.
translations.idiomatic: Elevated as outstanding talent, he governed Suyi.

sentences.zh: 左馮翊薛宣奏賞能治劇，徙為頻陽令，坐殘賊免。

translations.literal: Zuo Fengyi Xue Xuan memorialized Shang could govern severity—transferred chief of Pinyang—sat cruel predator dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: Xue Xuan sent him to rough Pinyang until cruelty ended his post.

sentences.zh: 後以御史舉為鄭令。

translations.literal: Later by censor raised chief of Zheng.
translations.idiomatic: The censor later restored him to Zheng.

sentences.zh: 永始、元延間，上怠於政，貴戚驕恣，紅陽長仲兄弟交通輕俠，臧匿亡命。

translations.literal: Between Yongshi and Yuanyan—sovereign slack in governance—noble affines arrogant wilful—Hongyang Zhang Zhong brothers trafficked with roaming knights—harbored fugitives.
translations.idiomatic: Under Cheng Di nobles armed gangsters and hid bandits.

sentences.zh: 而北地大豪浩商等報怨，殺義渠長妻子六人，往來長安中。

translations.literal: Meanwhile northern magnates like Hao Shang avenged grudges—killed Yiqu chief's wife and six children—coming-going Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: Northerners like Hao Shang murdered officials' families in the capital streets.

sentences.zh: 丞相、御史遣掾求逐黨與，詔書召捕，久之乃得。

translations.literal: Chancellor and imperial clerk dispatched clerks seeking chasing factions—edict summoned capture—long time then obtained.
translations.idiomatic: High ministers hunted the gangs until edicts bagged them.

sentences.zh: 長安中奸猾浸多，閭里少年群輩殺吏，受賕報仇，相與探丸為彈，得赤丸者斫武吏，得黑丸者斫文吏，白者主治喪；

translations.literal: Within Chang'an crafty multiplied—lane youths in gangs killed clerks—received bribes avenged—mutually drew pellets for bullets—getting red pellet hacked martial clerks—getting black hacked civil clerks—white governed funerals;
translations.idiomatic: Youth drew colored pellets to assign murders of bailiffs or scribes—white meant funeral duty;

sentences.zh: 城中薄暮塵起，剽劫行者，死傷橫道，枹鼓不絕。

translations.literal: At dusk dust rose in city—plundering travelers—dead wounded sprawled road—watch drums unceasing.
translations.idiomatic: Dusk brought drumbeats, dust, and corpses across avenues.

sentences.zh: 賞以三輔高第選守長安令，得一切便宜從事。

translations.literal: Shang by capital region top rank selected guard chief of Chang'an—obtained all convenient advantage.
translations.idiomatic: Top scores earned him Chang'an magistrate with carte blanche.

sentences.zh: 賞至，修治長安獄，穿地方深各數丈，致令辟為郭，以大石覆其口，名為「虎穴」。

translations.literal: Shang arrived—repaired Chang'an jail—dug earth square deep each several zhang—ordered rammed encircling wall—great stones covered mouth—named "Tiger Pit".
translations.idiomatic: He carved pits dubbed tiger dens beneath stone lids.

sentences.zh: 乃部戶曹掾史，與鄉吏、亭長、里正、父老、伍人，雜舉長安中輕薄少年惡子，無市籍商販作務，而鮮衣凶服被鎧扞持刀兵者，悉籍記之，得數百人。

translations.literal: Then departmental clerks with village clerks, pavilion chiefs, lane heads, elders, squad men—mixed raised Chang'an's flashy vicious youths—no market registry merchants craftsmen—in bright clothes mourning gear wearing armor grasping blades—all registered—several hundred obtained.
translations.idiomatic: He catalogued hundreds of armored rowdies without licenses.

sentences.zh: 賞一朝會長安吏，車數百輛，分行收捕，皆劾以為通行飲食群盜。

translations.literal: Shang one morning convened Chang'an clerks—carts several hundred—split lines seize arrest—all impeached as supplying passage food gang robbers.
translations.idiomatic: Hundreds of carts swept them up as rebel accomplices.

sentences.zh: 賞親閱，見十置一，其餘盡以次內虎穴中，百人為輩，覆以大石。

translations.literal: Shang personally reviewed—saw ten spare one—rest all in turn placed tiger pit—hundred men per batch—covered great stones.
translations.idiomatic: He spared one in ten and buried the rest alive under slabs.

sentences.zh: 數日一發視，皆相枕藉死，便輿出，瘞寺門桓東。

translations.literal: Several days once opened inspect—all pillow-stacked dead—carted out—buried east of lodge gate pillar.
translations.idiomatic: Days later he opened the pits, carted corpses to bury east of the gate.

sentences.zh: 榜著其姓名，百日後，乃令死者家各自發取其屍。

translations.literal: Posted placards bearing their names—after hundred days—ordered dead families each exhume take corpses.
translations.idiomatic: Placards named the dead; families dug them up after a hundred days.

sentences.zh: 親屬號哭，道路皆歔欷。

translations.literal: Kin wailed—roads all sob-sighed.
translations.idiomatic: Kin wailed until streets echoed with sobs.

sentences.zh: 長安中歌之曰：「安所求子死？

translations.literal: Within Chang'an sang: "Where seek your death?"
translations.idiomatic: Ballads asked where sons went to die:

sentences.zh: 桓東少年場。

translations.literal: East of pillar youths' yard.
translations.idiomatic: East of the lodge pillar—the youths' killing ground.

sentences.zh: 生時諒不謹，枯骨後何葬？

translations.literal: Living times indeed not cautious—dry bones afterward how bury?"
translations.idiomatic: Live reckless, leave bones unclaimed."

sentences.zh: 」賞所置皆其魁宿，或故吏善家子失計隨輕黠願自改者，財數十百人，皆貰其罪，詭令立功以自贖。

translations.literal: "Those Shang spared were their chieftains—or former clerks good-house sons misled following clever youths wishing reform—several tens hundred—all lent their crimes—secretly ordered merit to redeem.
translations.idiomatic: Survivors were chiefs or penitents drafted as his hounds.

sentences.zh: 盡力有效者，因親用之為爪牙，追捕甚精，甘耆奸惡，甚於凡吏。

translations.literal: Those who exhausted themselves effectively were then employed intimately as his claws—pursuit and capture became extremely keen—they relished hunting villains—more than ordinary clerks.
translations.idiomatic: His chosen survivors pursued felons with relish, outdoing normal bailiffs.

sentences.zh: 賞視事數月，盜賊止，郡國亡命散走，各歸其處，不敢窺長安。

translations.literal: Shang holding office several months—robbers ceased—commanderies kingdoms fugitives scattered fled—each returned place—dared not spy Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: Within months outlaws fled the capital.

sentences.zh: 江湖中多盜賊，以常為江夏太守，捕格江賊及所誅吏民甚多，坐殘賊免。

translations.literal: Mid-rivers many robbers—by permanent assignment Jiangxia grand administrator—captured fought river bandits—and executed clerks people many—sat cruel predator dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: River duty brought mass killings until cruelty cost him office.

sentences.zh: 南山群盜起，以賞為右輔都尉，遷執金吾，督大奸猾。

translations.literal: Southern Mountain gang robbers arose—by Shang as right metropolitan aide commandant—promoted Bearer of the Mace—supervised great crafty.
translations.idiomatic: Southern Mountain revolt won him right capital command then imperial guard chief.

sentences.zh: 三輔吏民甚畏之。

translations.literal: Capital region clerks and people greatly feared him.
translations.idiomatic: The tri-capital feared him deeply.

sentences.zh: 數年卒官。

translations.literal: Several years died in office.
translations.idiomatic: He died in harness within years.

sentences.zh: 疾病且死，戒其諸子曰：「丈夫為吏，正坐殘賊免，追思其功效，則復進用矣。

translations.literal: Ill nearing death—warned sons: "Husband serving as clerk—justly sits cruel predator dismissed—reflecting achievements—then again promoted.
translations.idiomatic: He told sons dismissal for cruelty beats softness forever.

sentences.zh: 一坐軟弱不勝任免，終身廢棄無有赦時，其羞辱甚於貪污坐臧。

translations.literal: One sitting weak unable dismissed—lifetime abandoned without amnesty moment—its shame surpasses greedy stolen wealth.
translations.idiomatic: Weak dismissal stains a lineage worse than graft.

sentences.zh: 慎毋然！

translations.literal: Careful not thus!"
translations.idiomatic: Never choose weakness!"

sentences.zh: 」賞四子皆至郡守，長子立為京兆尹，皆尚威嚴，有治辦名。

translations.literal: "Shang four sons all reached grand administrators—eldest son Li Metropolitan Governor—all esteemed awe stern—had governance renown.
translations.idiomatic: Four sons became governors; eldest Li ruled the metropolis sternly.

sentences.zh: 贊曰：「自郅都以下皆以酷烈為聲，然都抗直，引是非，爭大體。

translations.literal: Appraisal says: "From Zhi Du downward all took fierce severity as fame—yet Du resisted straight—cited right and wrong—contended great principle.
translations.idiomatic: The historian praises Zhi Du's blunt integrity amid cruelty.

sentences.zh: 張湯以知阿邑人主，與俱上下，時辯當否，國家賴其便。

translations.literal: Zhang Tang by knowing how to flatter lord—rising falling with him—sometimes debating fit or not—state relied on his convenience.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Tang curried the emperor yet kept the machinery turning.

sentences.zh: 趙禹據法守正。

translations.literal: Zhao Yu grasped law upheld rectitude.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Yu held fast to statute.

sentences.zh: 杜周從諛，以少言為重。

translations.literal: Du Zhou followed flattery—taking few words as weight.
translations.idiomatic: Du Zhou whispered assent and weighed silence.

sentences.zh: 張湯死後，罔密事叢，浸以耗廢，九卿奉職，救過不給，何暇論繩墨之外乎！

translations.literal: After Zhang Tang died—nets dense affairs clustered—gradually wasted—Nine Ministers holding duty—saving faults insufficient—what leisure discuss ink-line beyond!
translations.idiomatic: After Zhang Tang the legal net tangled ministers who could barely plug leaks.

sentences.zh: 自是以至哀、平，酷吏眾多，然莫足數，此其知名見紀者也。

translations.literal: From this reaching Ai and Ping—cruel clerks numerous—yet none worth counting—these are famous recorded.
translations.idiomatic: From Ai to Ping cruel clerks multiplied beyond counting—only these merit record.

sentences.zh: 其廉者足以為儀表，其污者方略教道，一切禁奸，亦質有文武焉。

translations.literal: Among them incorrupt enough be model—corrupt ones still deployed stratagem teaching—wholesale forbidding wicked—also substance possessed civil martial.
translations.idiomatic: The honest set examples; the corrupt still suppressed crime—both strains had their place.

sentences.zh: 雖酷，稱其位矣。

translations.literal: Though cruel—they matched their posts.
translations.idiomatic: Even cruelty fit its office.

sentences.zh: 湯、周子孫貴盛，故別傳。

translations.literal: Tang and Zhou descendants noble flourishing—therefore separate biography.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Tang's and Du Zhou's prosperous heirs earn separate chapters.
